items.headline: City approves Seeds of Peace Monument at Guadalupe River Park
items.summary: San José City Council approved an agreement with The Diane Brandenburg Survivor's Trust for the installation of the Seeds of Peace Monument, an artwork by Mario Chiodo, at Guadalupe River Park Arena Green West. The monument will be a permanent public art installation at the popular riverside park.

items.headline: Council changes budget rules for mayoral and district offices
items.summary: City Council proposed changes to Policy 1-18, Section 20, governing how the Office of the Mayor and City Council District Office budgets are handled during transition years. The policy change will affect how these offices manage funding when leadership changes occur.

items.headline: Tully Road Eastridge Business Improvement District approves 2026-27 budget
items.summary: City Council took action on the Tully Road Eastridge Business Improvement District's 2026-2027 Budget Report and Annual Assessment. The BID, which covers the commercial corridor, approved its funding plan for the coming fiscal year.

items.headline: Cupertino updates Health and Safety Element of General Plan
items.summary: The City Council is updating the Health and Safety Element of the Cupertino General Plan covering 2015–2040. This foundational planning document shapes how the city addresses public health, emergency preparedness, and safety risks over the next 15 years.

items.headline: City Council appoints permanent City Manager
items.summary: The council adopted a resolution appointing a permanent City Manager. This fills the leadership role responsible for day-to-day city operations and implementing council directives.

items.headline: Council considers urgency ordinance adding new municipal code section
items.summary: The council held a public hearing and considered adopting an urgency ordinance that would add a new section to Milpitas municipal code. The urgency designation allows faster enactment than standard procedures.

items.headline: Site development permit amendment and tentative map approved
items.summary: The council adopted a resolution approving a site development permit amendment and vesting tentative map, affecting a development project in the city.

items.headline: Duplex construction permitted on Old Piedmont Road
items.summary: A new two-story, 2-unit duplex has been permitted at 2174 Old Piedmont Rd ($1M+), representing one of the larger residential projects in this permit batch. This marks new multi-unit housing rather than the single-unit ADUs dominating recent approvals.
